dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the ritual failed. Or rather, something fought back.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: When you placed the {quest_item} at the shrine, it absorbed the offering and grew stronger. {target_npc} says the corruption has a will of its own: it's feeding on our attempts to purify it. The old texts mention a second shrine deeper in the temple {location_hint}, an anchor for the curse. The {enemy_type} presence has tripled. {quest_threat} knows we're coming.
dialogueChunks.outro: We're not just fighting corruption anymore. {quest_focus} is more dangerous than we imagined, and it was old before this settlement existed.

playerResponses.text: Then we go deeper. It can't hold on forever.
A contest of wills with an ancient curse. This keeps getting better.
If the ritual made it worse, I'm done making things worse.

dialogueChunks.intro: The corruption has reached {location}, {player_name}. We're running out of time.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The blackened earth is creeping toward the walls. Trees are splitting open. {target_npc} collapsed this morning: {quest_stakes} is in the air now. The only option is to gather massive quantities of {quest_item} and create a barrier ward while someone returns to the source. The {enemy_type} are everywhere, drawn to the energy like moths to flame.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_threat} wants us gone from this land. We have to make our stand or abandon everything.

playerResponses.text: Then we make our stand. I'll gather what we need and face the source.
Angry ancient forces. Exactly what I signed up for.
If the land itself wants you gone, maybe you should listen.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the spirit guardians spoke. Not in words: in visions. And what they showed me changes everything.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The corruption wasn't random. Someone deliberately broke the shrine seals generations ago. {quest_focus} have been trying to contain the damage ever since, growing desperate and violent. They're not our enemies: they're exhausted protectors. {target_npc} found records {location_hint} describing a binding ritual requiring rare {quest_item}. Someone must carry it through territory the {enemy_type} have overrun.
dialogueChunks.outro: We've been fighting the wrong battle. {quest_threat} was never directed at us: it was a cry for help.

playerResponses.text: Then we help them. Show me where to find what we need.
Generations of damage to undo. No pressure.
Visions and binding rituals are beyond what I can offer.
